message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Mariscos El Capitan (Coachella)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Google ~1,100+ reviews (4.2 stars); Yelp ~186-192 reviews (3.5 stars); Restaurant Guru ~1,800 votes; Facebook ~490 reviews
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: Operating since approximately 2015 (~10-11 years); website copyright dates to 2019; ownership stability [UNVERIFIED] beyond family-owned positioning
- Credentials: ["Featured in Palm Springs Life '6 Must-Try Restaurants in the City of Coachella'","Listed on Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism directory","Ranked #2 of 41 seafood restaurants in Coachella per Restaurant Guru aggregation"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best mariscos Coachella', 'seafood Coachella CA', 'Mexican seafood Coachella Valley'. Does NOT reliably surface for valley-wide queries like 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'date night Coachella Valley' where Palm Desert / La Quinta / Palm Springs venues dominate. Appears in Palm Springs Life Coachella city roundup and Visit Greater Palm Springs listings.
- Site structured data: Weak. Site is a brochure-style WordPress build by 'New Emage' with no detectable schema.org/JSON-LD markup, no machine-readable hours on the homepage, menus are PDF-only (not crawlable as structured data), and no LocalBusiness/Restaurant schema. Social links present but no structured location signal beyond plain-text address.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

## Overview
Mariscos El Capitan was established in 2015 and is located at 52565 Cesar Chavez St, Coachella, CA 92236.

I found relevant search results about Mariscos El Capitan in Coachella. Here's what I discovered:

## Overview

Mariscos El Capitan in Coachella is known for its authentic mariscos (Mexican seafood), from fresh ceviches and shrimp cocktails to whole fish and house specialties.

## Recognition as a Must-Try Restaurant

Baja-style mariscos dominate the menu at this colorful Latino-owned restaurant located between the Pueblo Viejo downtown district and Bagdouma Park. The casual concept started as a food trailer in Bakersfield, expanding to a brick-and-mortar there in 2018, then opening a second outpost in Coachella in 2023.

## Menu Highlights

The menu features beachside classics such as aguachile, campechanos, and tacos, with signature chavelas crafted with a special housemade mix.

## Atmosphere & Entertainment

What sets it apart is its energetic, community-driven vibe, with live Banda music transforming dinner into a full-on night out, featuring live music every Thursday, Friday, and Sunday starting at 7 p.m.

## Contact Information

Located at 52565 Cesar Chavez St, Suite 101, Coachella, CA 92236, the restaurant can be reached at (760) 600-7575.

content.content:
# Mariscos El Capitan in Visit Greater Palm Springs Directory

Based on the provided webpage content, **no detailed listing information for Mariscos El Capitan is available**. While the page title mentions "Mariscos El Capitan | Coachella, CA," the actual page content consists only of navigation menus, header elements, footer information, and sponsor logos from the Visit Greater Palm Springs tourism site.

The webpage does not display any restaurant details, category information, descriptions, or other specifics about this establishment. To find comprehensive information about Mariscos El Capitan, you would need to access the full restaurant listing page directly or navigate through the site's "Restaurants & Nightlife" section.

# Mariscos El Capitan - Structured Data Analysis

## Schema/Microdata Findings

**No JSON-LD or schema.org markup detected.** The page lacks:
- Restaurant schema
- LocalBusiness schema
- Microdata attributes
- Structured hours data

## Copyright & Date Information

**Copyright Year:** 2019
- Statement: "Company 2019 © All Rights Reserved"

## Menu Availability

**Yes, menu is PDF-only.** Two options provided:
1. Sushi Menu PDF (dated 2021)
2. Web Menu PDF (dated 2026 — likely a typo for 2025)

No inline menu content or HTML-based menu listings appear on the website.

## Contact Information

- **Phone:** (760) 600-7575
- **Address:** 52565 Harrison St. 92236 Coachella
- **Social:** Facebook and Instagram links present

## Recommendation

The site would benefit significantly from adding Restaurant schema markup (schema.org) to improve SEO, local search visibility, and knowledge panel eligibility. Hours of operation are notably absent from both the page content and structured data.

content.text: Note the website lists the address as Harrison St, not Cesar Chavez St as Yelp shows. Let me check the Palm Springs Life article via search and verify a few more claims.

Based on the search results, here are the best mariscos (Mexican seafood) restaurants in the Coachella Valley:

## Top-Rated Mariscos Restaurants

Some of the most highly-rated options include Mariscos El Capitan, Mr Clamato, Mariscoco's Culiacan, La Mango Botanas And Beer, and Sushi y Mariscos Guamuchil.

### Notable Restaurants:

**Mariscos El Capitan** - This seafood-centric restaurant offers a beach-inspired atmosphere with mariachi music and specializes in oyster shots, molcajetes, and ceviche, along with Mexican-style sushi. The restaurant features live music on weekends with Mexican bands.

**El Tranvia** - This colorful Latino-owned restaurant features Baja-style mariscos and expanded from a food trailer in Bakersfield to a brick-and-mortar location in Coachella in 2023, offering beachside classics such as aguachile, campechanos, and tacos.

**Mariscoco's Culiacan** - This restaurant focuses on authentic Mexican flavors with really fresh products and a wide variety of dishes.

**Sushi y Mariscos Guamuchil** - This spot offers fresh seafood, sushi, and delicious tacos with bold flavors and authentic recipes from the coast.

These restaurants are particularly popular for their fresh seafood offerings, vibrant atmosphere, and authentic Mexican coastal cuisine.

I found the article you were looking for! 

Mariscos El Capitán is a colorful Latino-owned restaurant that specializes in Baja-style mariscos, which started as a food trailer in Bakersfield and opened its Coachella location in 2023. The menu features beachside classics such as aguachile, campechanos, and tacos, and their signature chavela is crafted with a special housemade mix.

For those with Sunday hangovers, El Capitán serves a hot bowl of menudo and fresh tortillas along with shrimp-stuffed chavelás. The restaurant features live music with large ensemble bands that bring in an energized crowd.

This restaurant is featured in the Palm Springs Life article titled "6 Must-Try Restaurants in the City of Coachella."

I found several excellent date night restaurant options in the Coachella Valley, including Palm Desert and La Quinta. Here are some top recommendations:

## Romantic Fine Dining

Morgan's in the Desert is a perfect spot for a date night with its elegant and romantic ambiance, featuring 'exquisite surroundings' and 'old world Spanish charm'.

Mastro's Palm Desert is an OpenTable Diners' Choice winner for 2025 and a top destination for unforgettable dining in the Coachella Valley.

## Steakhouses & Seafood

Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.

Eddie V's is touted as an excellent seafood restaurant in Palm Desert that also has the reputation of being a delectable steakhouse, serving fresh seafood and hand-cut steaks.

## Scenic Views

The La Quinta Cliffhouse is renowned for its spectacular views across the valley and provides a casual destination to enjoy American classics with innovative preparations focused on premium steaks and seafood.

## Other Notable Options

Lavender Bistro in La Quinta and Coral Sushi & Sake Bar, which offers fresh sushi and specialty rolls in a trendy, vibrant atmosphere with an outdoor covered patio, are also popular choices.

content.text: The Palm Springs Life "2023 opening / food trailer Bakersfield" detail came from search snippets twice — but the same description was also applied to "El Tranvia" in one result. There's confusion. The website copyright is 2019 (predates 2023), suggesting the restaurant existed before then. The conflicting claims suggest the snippet may have misattributed El Tranvia's history to El Capitan. Either way, the 2015 founding claim cannot be cleanly confirmed.

confirms: Yelp review count ~186-192 with 3.5 stars (Yelp result confirms 186 reviews, other sources cite 192)
Restaurant Guru aggregated reviews/votes in the ~1,700-1,800 range (1,777 reviews confirmed)
Featured/tagged in Palm Springs Life coverage of Coachella restaurants (dedicated tag page exists; '6 Must-Try Restaurants in the City of Coachella' references El Capitan)
Listed on Visit Greater Palm Springs official tourism directory (dedicated listing page at /listing/mariscos-el-capitan/35625/)

Site structured data is weak: no JSON-LD or schema.org markup detected, menus are PDF-only, copyright dates to 2019, no machine-readable hours on homepage, no LocalBusiness/Restaurant schema (independently verified by fetching the homepage)
Surfaces strongly for narrow queries like 'best mariscos Coachella' and 'Mexican seafood Coachella Valley' (appears in Yelp top-10 mariscos results and Restaurant Guru aggregation)

Does NOT surface in valley-wide 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'date night Coachella Valley' queries — those results are dominated by Palm Desert/La Quinta venues (Morgan's, Mastro's, Eddie V's, Chez Pierre, Lavender Bistro, etc.)
contradicts: Address inconsistency: the official site lists '52565 Harrison St' while Yelp/Visit Greater Palm Springs/Palm Springs Life list '52565 Cesar Chavez St, Suite 101' — the site's own address is incorrect or outdated, which itself is a visibility/data-quality problem not captured in the assessment

Possible longevity conflict: one Palm Springs Life article snippet describes the Coachella location opening in 2023 (originating as a food trailer in Bakersfield, brick-and-mortar Bakersfield 2018, Coachella outpost 2023) — this conflicts with the 'operating since approximately 2015 (~10-11 years)' claim. NOTE: that snippet may have conflated El Capitan with El Tranvia in the same roundup; the website copyright of 2019 is consistent with pre-2023 existence. The 2015 founding date is not cleanly supported by primary sources and should be downgraded.
flagged_as_unverified: Google ~1,100+ reviews at 4.2 stars — could not independently verify exact Google review count from a primary Google source (Yelp/Restaurant Guru confirmed but Google count is secondary-source only)
Facebook ~490 reviews — Facebook page exists (two pages found: MariscosElCapitanPaginaOficial and MariscoselCapitanCoachella) but exact review count not independently verified
Specific ranking '#2 of 41 seafood restaurants in Coachella per Restaurant Guru' — Restaurant Guru aggregation confirmed but exact rank position not visible in search results

Founding year of 2015 — repeated in some secondary sources but no primary documentation (official 'About Us' page is template/empty per search results); website copyright is 2019; Palm Springs Life snippet suggests 2023 Coachella opening — actual founding year ambiguous
Family-owned positioning and ownership stability — described as 'family restaurant' in secondary sources but no primary verification of ownership history
input.notes: Structured-data assessment is solid and independently confirmed (no JSON-LD, PDF menus, 2019 copyright, no schema). Review-volume and credential claims largely hold up. The weakest link is the longevity claim — '2015, ~10-11 years' is repeated in secondary aggregators but a Palm Springs Life snippet describes a 2023 Coachella opening (possibly conflated with El Tranvia in the same article). The official website's address (Harrison St) conflicts with the address every directory lists (Cesar Chavez St), which is itself a meaningful agent-visibility data-quality problem the assessment missed. Visibility gap score of 6 and 'convertible' tier seem reasonable given the verified structured-data gaps. Sources: yelp.com/biz/mariscos-el-capitan-coachella, restaurantguru.com/Mariscos-El-Capitan-Coachella, palmspringslife.com/restaurants/6-must-try-restaurants-in-the-city-of-coachella/, palmspringslife.com/tag/mariscos-el-capitan/, visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/mariscos-el-capitan/35625/, mariscoselcapitancoachella.com/.
